title: Port shell and scripting grammars: Zsh, Ruby, Perl, Java, HTML, CSS
description:
Generate grammar packages and port external scanners for the remaining target languages.

These follow the established pattern from earlier grammar ports.

Languages:
- Zsh: separate grammar from Bash, scanner extends Bash concepts (must be done after Bash)
- Ruby: external scanner for heredocs, string interpolation
- Perl: may have limited tree-sitter grammar maturity
- Java: relatively straightforward grammar
- HTML: exercises language injection (SetIncludedRanges) with embedded JS/CSS
- CSS: commonly injected within HTML

Deliverables per grammar:
- Generated grammar package via tsgo-generate
- External scanner ported to Go
- Differential test passing against C tree-sitter
- HTML/CSS: validate language injection with SetIncludedRanges

Tests:
- Parse real-world files for each language
- Differential test against C tree-sitter
- HTML: test language injection with embedded JS and CSS blocks

Reference: docs/design.md §4, §5
